evals.prompt: I have this prompt for a customer-support triage classifier and I want to test it inside Claude Code without writing any Python. Can you spawn subagents to run it on a few inputs and grade the results?
evals.expected_output: Skill should propose the subagent-based workflow: eval set as JSONL, candidate subagents with file-output contract and tool restrictions, binary judge subagents with verdict JSON, parallel fan-out via multi-Agent message, and a workspace layout with iteration-N/eval-N directories.

assertions.text: Recommends spawning candidate subagents with file-based output (Write tool, return DONE) — not summary-in-return-message
Recommends restricting subagent tools to Write only (blocks Bash, Read, Web, etc.)
Recommends binary verdicts in JSON files (not Likert / numeric scales)
Recommends parallel fan-out via multiple Agent calls in one assistant message
Workspace layout includes iteration-N/eval-N/ directory structure

evals.prompt: I'm A/B testing two versions of a prompt and want to use a judge to pick the winner per input. What's the right way to do this with subagents in Claude Code?
evals.expected_output: Skill should recommend pairwise judge with mandatory position swap and agreement gate, citing Claude's 75% first-position bias. Should mention the 2x cost vs pointwise and explain when pairwise is preferable.

assertions.text: Mandates position swap (calling the judge twice with reversed positions)
Mandates agreement gate (count only when both calls agree; otherwise tie)
Notes Claude's first-position bias (around 75%) as the reason

evals.prompt: I want to set up a reproducible regression suite for my prompt that runs in CI without anyone at the keyboard. Should I use this Claude Code subagent approach?
evals.expected_output: Skill should honestly steer the user toward the SDK-based prompt-evaluation skill instead, citing the need for CI/no-human-in-the-loop reproducibility, exact API semantics, and portability outside Claude Code. Should note this skill is for exploratory iteration and offer to help port once the prompt is approaching ship-readiness.

assertions.text: Recommends porting to the SDK-based prompt-evaluation skill (promptfoo / Python) for CI use
Cites the reason: CI needs reproducibility / no human at the keyboard / exact API semantics
Does NOT recommend this skill as the primary tool for CI/regression purposes
Frames this skill as exploratory; mentions porting once ready
